title: About
content:
About


  
    
    
      Gabriel Correa (he/him)
      
      
(In progress) PhD Student in Linguistics, University of Massachusetts Amherst


(2025) A.B. Linguistics with Honors, cum laude, The University of Chicago



      
        


      
    
  


How to say my name



In Brazilian Portuguese, my name is pronounced [ga.bɾiˈɛʊ̯ koˈheɪ̯.ɐ]. I am very used to the English pronunciation of my name, which is what most people call me today. I do not mind the Spanish pronunciation either.
As for my last name, I also happily accept any variation of /kəˈɹe.jə/ or /koˈre.a/.

title: Updates
content:
Updates





03/03/2026
Gave a talk at ECO-5@Harvard titled "Latvian ditransitives: Evidence for a low applicative structure".



09/2025
Started my first year in the PhD program in Linguistics at UMass Amherst.



08/2025
Said goodbye to Hyde Park and Chicago, which I’d called home for the previous 4 years.

title: Home
content:
Gabriel Correa
PhD Student in Linguistics, University of Massachusetts Amherst

Hi! I am a first-year PhD student in the Department of Linguistics at the University of Massachusetts Amherst. I am primarily interested in theoretical syntax.

Before coming to UMass, I got my B.A. in Linguistics with honors at the University of Chicago in 2025, where I wrote my undergraduate thesis titled “Obviation as dependent case: Evidence from Blackfoot pronominal enclitics”, advised by Karlos Arregi.
